sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal, i.q. כָּסַל No. 3, Syr. and Zab. ܣܟܰܠ to be foolish, which must be carefully distinguished from the verb, which is similar in sound, שָׂכַל.

Piel, to make foolish, i.e. vain, fruitless, to frustrate, e.g. a counsel or purpose, 2Sa 15:31 Isa 44:25. Compare הֹולֵל.

Hiphil, to act foolishly, with the addition of עֲשֹׂו Gen 31:28 without it, 1Sa 26:21. (Aram. Aph. id.)

Niphal-

(1) to act foolishly (prop. to shew oneself foolish), 1Sa 13:13 2Ch 16:9.

(2) to act wickedly, 2Sa 24:10 1Ch 21:8, comp. כְּסִיל, נָבָל, etc. Hence [the following words.]
